summary: A small two-story rectangular stone building on a raised cobblestone perimeter platform with rows of glass windows on both floors and a dark red sloped roof capped with light-colored slabs at the gable ends.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with a pitched roof centered on top and a surrounding low perimeter platform
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (mirrored window rows, roof shape)

elements: two floors with horizontal window rows (four-to-five windows per side)
raised cobblestone perimeter platform surrounding base (~1 block higher than ground)
nether-brick sloped gable roof with stepped edges and light slabs at the top
stonebrick wall detailing band between floors and corner pilasters
single centered entrance (uncertain interior door type) with small recessed porch
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular stonebrick box roughly 11x7 blocks footprint, 6 blocks tall for two floors (3 blocks per floor) plus roof and foundation.
Place a 1-block high cobblestone platform extending 1 block outward around the base.
Install continuous rows of glass panes on both long sides centered vertically (approximately 4–5 panes per row).
Create a nether-brick gable roof peaked along the long axis with 1-block stepped slope; cap the gable ends with light colored stone slabs.
Add a decorative stonebrick band (one block depth) between the first and second floor and stonebrick corner columns.
uncertainties: Exact internal floor heights and interior layout are not visible.
Precise footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from images and top-down views; could be off by a few blocks.
Material identification for roof trim appears to be a light stone slab (stone_slab) but could be another pale slab variant.
Entrance details and whether there is an interior staircase or second-floor access are unknown.
